Off Calendar Feb-25-2009 Continued To Mar-11-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-04-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: William Scherer in Propria Persona. Paul Melbostad for Jonathan Gove (Guardian Ad Litem for Samantha Gove).

The Parties agreed with the recommendation of the Court to place the funds into a blocked account in California with Jonathan Gove as custodian under CUTMA for Samantha Gove. The Court granted the petition, (signing the revised order for the same in open court). The funds are to be wire transferred directly to the blocked account. Status on filing of the receipt for the blocked account was set for February 25, 2009.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: R. Noguera/J. Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy J. Garrido.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jan-28-2009 Continued To Feb-04-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Interested Person or Counsel.

An Order To Show Cause will be issued to William Scherer (Executor in Propria Persona) for failure to appear and respond to the Examiner's notes. The return date is March 11, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Department 204.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy M. Svahlin.

Upon further review, the Court will not issue the Order to Show Cause. The matter was continued to February 4, 2009 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Nancy Levin for Andrew Wurmser. Charlene Johnson for William Scherer (Executor). Paul Melbostad for Stephen Yarbrough and Jonathan Gove.

The Court questioned the father signing for the Minor. Mr. Melbostad stated that Mr. Gove (Father) was actively involved in the case. The Court stated that it was not the policy of the court to approve the petition for a minor without a Guardian being appointed. Mr. Melbostad may file an ex parte petition for the court to consider the appointment of Mr. Gove. The matter was continued to April 2, 2008.
